pundit.name: Alexi Lalas
pundit.take: **Gunner, you drafted like a man who forgot the World Cup doesn't feature club teams — what, PSG wasn't available?** Devin, you may be leading this circus with a measly 4 points, but let me tell you something, you're the dullest blade in the drawer. Your pick of Bosnia over, say, ANYONE of actual consequence is what happens when the Ghost Pepper lacks punch. Let's shift our attention to "Bubba G" Gunner, who thought drafting DR Congo would unleash some secret weapon — you might as well have picked the Harlem Globetrotters! As for Zach, "Mustard Boy," are you feeling giddy about a tie between Brazil and Morocco? Sure, it gave you a point, but let me remind you, this is the World Cup, not a Sunday league. Watch out for Gayden, the "Backpass Assassin," holding steady with 3 points — he's lurking like a quiet predator. Rafe, "The Noisemaker," you’re somehow in third because chaos has a way of sticking around. But heed this, my little soccer rascals, it’s not about how you start, it’s how you flame out spectacularly. So tighten those cleats, because for most of you, this fantasy pool is already a sinking ship.

pundit_takes.headline: Zach's Brazil Fails to Win, Sinks to Last Place
pundit_takes.subtitle: Mustard Boy must learn draws don't add flavor to standings.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden Poised for a Tactical Triumph Against Netherlands Today
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Backpass Assassin plans lethal sideways passes to secure second.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner's Morocco Holds Brazil, But Still Trails in Standings
pundit_takes.subtitle: Bubba G's strategy: pick strong ties, sleep on the rest.

pundit_takes.headline: Devin Leads Standings Despite Ghost Pepper's Flaccid Draws
pundit_takes.subtitle: With a lineup as spicy as boiled potatoes, still on top.

rome_analytics_quotes: Rafe, 15 and clueless, still outshines Zach's veteran standings. Who's teaching who?
Zach, you depend on Brazil, who only got you 1 point so far. Ouch.
Rafe humbling Devin already? The kid's showing he's no pushover in family duels.
The sim still can't predict a 15-year-old blind-drafting underdog. Step up, tech.
